meta_title: For Founders: design with AI to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Escape Tutorial Hell for Founders
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Use AI-driven workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code to validate your SaaS idea and build your MVP faster.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you convert abstract ideas into validated, revenue-generating SaaS products. Focus on high-leverage AI design patterns that prioritize core business logic over endless syntax memorization.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'Build a To-Do List' tutorial, yet your bank account remains static. You are trapped in tutorial hell—a cognitive trap where the illusion of learning replaces the reality of shipping. You aren't building a product; you are curating a collection of abandoned GitHub repositories that will never see a paying customer.

It is time to pivot from passive observer to technical founder. By designing with AI, you bypass the friction of boilerplate and syntax, shifting your focus to the only metric that matters: market validation. We are stripping away the fluff to show you how to architect, code, and deploy your MVP using modern AI tooling.

steps.title: Define the Business Primitive
steps.description: Don't start with code. Start with the core value loop. Define the 'Job to be Done' so the AI knows exactly what to scaffold.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Create a minimal schema for a SaaS MVP solving [Problem] for [Target Audience]. Focus on the core transaction loop only.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate project directory structure based on the schema provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Scaffold with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. This prevents 'blank page syndrome' and establishes a professional project structure immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack Next.js project structure with Supabase integration and Tailwind CSS. Include standard authentication and a basic dashboard layout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project files based on Antigravity output and install necessary npm dependencies.'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation with Claude Code
steps.description: Instead of writing every line, prompt Claude Code to build features in vertical slices—from database to UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a user profile view that fetches data from the Supabase 'profiles' table. Use clean, modular components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the profile view component and update the database service layer to match.'

steps.title: Integrate Business Logic
steps.description: Inject your specific monetization logic. This is where your product becomes a business, not just a script.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add Stripe checkout logic to the dashboard. Ensure the subscription status is stored in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate Stripe API, set up webhook handler, and update database schema for subscription tracking.'

steps.title: Ship and Validate
steps.description: Deploy to a production environment immediately. Perfection is the enemy of revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for security vulnerabilities and optimize the build for Vercel deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run build tests and provide deployment instructions for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Treat AI as a junior developer: Never trust the first output; always ask for a critique of the generated code.
Focus on the 'Happy Path': Build the shortest route to a user paying you money, then iterate.
Use Claude Code's context awareness to keep your documentation in sync with your evolving schema.
Prioritize readability over performance in the MVP phase; you can optimize the database queries later once you have users.
Maintain a 'Feature Log' in your project root; use AI to update it every time you complete a task to prevent scope creep.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, focus exclusively on acquiring your first 10 users. Use their feedback to drive your next round of AI-assisted feature development.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start executing. The gap between an idea and a profitable SaaS is simply the discipline to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
